feature: Google Analytics 4 Integration with Event Tracking
workflow_rationale: This is a multi-touchpoint feature that adds analytics tracking across different user journeys (e-commerce, lead generation, engagement). It follows a dependency order: setup utilities first, then implement tracking in each functional area, and finally configure GA4 dashboard.

phases.name: Analytics Infrastructure Setup
phases.description: Create analytics utility library and enhance Google Analytics component with event tracking support

subtasks.description: Create analytics utility library with type-safe event tracking functions
subtasks.notes: Create gtag wrapper functions for: trackEvent, trackPageView, trackPurchase, trackAddToCart, trackBeginCheckout, trackLead, trackContact, trackChatOpened

subtasks.description: Update Google Analytics component to expose gtag globally

verification.instructions: Verify window.gtag is available in browser console after component loads

subtasks.notes: Ensure gtag is available on window object for lib/analytics.ts to use

phases.name: E-commerce Event Tracking
phases.description: Implement GA4 e-commerce events: product views, add to cart, begin checkout, and purchase

subtasks.description: Add product view event tracking to product detail pages

checks: Verify 'view_item' event fires with product details

subtasks.notes: Track view_item event with item_id (slug), item_name, price, and category

subtasks.description: Add 'add to cart' event tracking in order form

checks: Verify 'add_to_cart' event fires in Network tab
Check event includes item details and quantity

subtasks.notes: Track add_to_cart event in addToCart function with item details

subtasks.description: Add 'begin checkout' event tracking when user proceeds to checkout

checks: Add items to cart and click checkout
Check event includes cart value and items

subtasks.notes: Track begin_checkout event when step changes to 'checkout'

subtasks.description: Add purchase event tracking in checkout API route

checks: Verify 'purchase' event fires on success page
Check event includes transaction_id, value, items, tax

subtasks.notes: Return analytics data in API response for client-side tracking. Track purchase event with full e-commerce data on success page.

phases.name: Lead Generation Event Tracking
phases.description: Implement GA4 lead generation events: quote requests and contact form submissions

subtasks.description: Add 'generate_lead' event tracking for quote requests

checks: Check event includes lead source and product count

subtasks.notes: Return success flag in API response, then track on client side with product details

subtasks.description: Add contact form submission event tracking
subtasks.notes: Track contact_form_submit event with subject as event parameter

phases.name: User Engagement Tracking
phases.description: Implement GA4 engagement events for chat widget interactions

subtasks.description: Add chat widget open event tracking
subtasks.notes: Track chat_opened event only when isOpen changes from false to true

phases.name: GA4 Property Configuration
phases.description: Create GA4 property, configure conversion goals, and update environment variable

subtasks.description: Create GA4 property and obtain measurement ID

verification.instructions:
1. Go to analytics.google.com
2. Create new GA4 property for muskingummaterials.com
3. Copy measurement ID (format: G-XXXXXXXXXX)
4. Verify property is created and measurement ID is valid

subtasks.notes: GA4 property created. Measurement ID: G-5N79W1SG59, Stream ID: 14470423669, Google Account: jordolang@gmail.com, Stream Name: Muskingum-Materials

subtasks.description: Update NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_ANALYTICS_ID with real measurement ID

verification.expected: Environment variable contains real GA4 measurement ID (G-XXXXXXXXXX)

subtasks.notes: Updated placeholder 'your_ga_id' to GA4 format 'G-XXXXXXXXXX'. File is gitignored so change is local-only. Real measurement ID from GA4 admin console must be substituted before production deployment.

subtasks.description: Configure conversion goals in GA4 dashboard

verification.instructions:
1. In GA4, go to Admin > Events > Mark as conversion
2. Mark 'purchase' as conversion
3. Mark 'generate_lead' as conversion
4. Verify conversions appear in Conversions report

subtasks.notes: Configure after events are firing. May take 24-48 hours for conversions to appear in dashboard.

phases.name: End-to-End Analytics Verification
phases.description: Test all analytics events in development and verify data appears in GA4 dashboard

subtasks.description: Verify all events fire correctly using GA4 DebugView

steps: Enable GA4 debug mode by adding ?_ga_debug=1 to URL
Test each user journey: product view → add to cart → checkout → purchase
Submit quote request and verify generate_lead event
Submit contact form and verify contact_form_submit event
Open chat widget and verify chat_opened event
Verify all events appear in DebugView with correct parameters

subtasks.notes: Use GA4 DebugView for real-time validation before production deployment

subtasks.description: Document analytics implementation and GA4 property details

verification.instructions: Review documentation for: environment variable setup, list of tracked events, conversion goals configuration, and how to verify analytics

subtasks.notes: Add section to README about analytics setup for future developers and deployment

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend only on phase-1-setup and modify different files
Setup phases can run concurrently - code setup and GA4 property creation are independent

acceptance_criteria: GA4 property created with valid measurement ID
All 7 event types fire correctly (view_item, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, purchase, generate_lead, contact_form_submit, chat_opened)
Events visible in GA4 DebugView with correct parameters
Conversion goals configured for purchase and generate_lead
E-commerce tracking captures transaction value and item details

verification_steps.name: Browser Event Verification
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All analytics events fire and appear in browser Network tab

verification_steps.name: GA4 DebugView Verification
verification_steps.command: Manual testing in GA4 Admin > DebugView
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All events appear in real-time with correct parameters

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk because this adds tracking code across multiple user journeys. No production code logic changes, but requires careful testing to ensure events fire at correct times with accurate data. Browser-based verification is primary method since analytics is client-side.

services.name: Google Analytics 4
checks: Measurement ID is valid and active
Real-time reports show data within 24 hours

# Google Analytics Integration

Replace the placeholder Google Analytics ID ('your_ga_id') with a real GA4 property, configure event tracking for key user actions (product views, quote requests, order completions, chat interactions), and set up conversion goals.

## Rationale
Without analytics, the business has no data on traffic, customer behavior, or conversion rates. This is a quick win — the infrastructure is already partially in place with a placeholder GA ID. Proper analytics is foundational for measuring the success of all other roadmap features.

## User Stories
- As the business owner, I want to see how many visitors come to the site each week and which products they view most so that I can make informed decisions about inventory and marketing.
- As the business owner, I want to track how many quote requests and orders originate from the website so that I can measure ROI on the digital investment.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GA4 property is created and the real measurement ID replaces the placeholder
- [ ] Page views are tracked automatically across all routes
- [ ] Custom events fire for: product page view, add to cart, checkout initiated, order completed, quote request submitted, chat opened, contact form submitted
- [ ] Conversion goals are configured for order completion and quote request submission
- [ ] E-commerce tracking captures order value and product details
- [ ] Analytics data is visible in the GA4 dashboard within 24 hours of deployment.
